summary: Matched Kirby's copied Fox/Falco blaster special code in ftkirbyspecialfox.c. The PR implemented ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30, completed ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240, and centralized several ftKb_Init data declarations. decomp.dev reported +1040 matched bytes, +0.03% overall matched code, 3 new function matches, and an .sdata2 improvement. Review focused on avoiding an obviously artificial goto-based match and preserving proper OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage.

key_files.role: Implemented the Kirby Fox/Falco blaster shot function and adjusted ground/air start functions to reach 100% matches.
Added shared declarations for ftKb_Init_804D3DB8, ftKb_Init_804D3DC0, ftKb_Init_804D9478, and ftKb_Init_804D9480.

what_changed: Replaced the placeholder for ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 with a real implementation; ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot remains as a wrapper that calls ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30.

ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 now checks fp->cmd_vars[2], clears it, computes a blaster shot position from FtPart_R3rdNa via lb_8000B1CC using offsets 0.0f, 1.45f, and 5.016f, zeros sp2C.z, then branches on fp->fv.kb.hat.kind.

For FTKIND_FOX and FTKIND_FALCO, the new code selects the corresponding ftKb_DatAttrs launch angle, launch speed, projectile id, and SFX array; it mirrors the launch angle with M_PI - angle when facing left, calls it_8029C6A4, calls it_802AE1D0 on fp->fv.kb.xB0, and plays SFX with ft_PlaySFX.
Removed inline helper ftKbGetBlasterId and instead computes blaster_id locally in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240 before calling it_802AE8A8.
Added includes for ft/ft_0877.h and it/items/itfoxlaser.h to support newly referenced functions/items.
Moved ftKb_Init_804D9478 and ftKb_Init_804D9480 declarations out of the C file into ftKb_Init.h, and added header declarations for the two u32 SFX arrays ftKb_Init_804D3DB8 and ftKb_Init_804D3DC0.

decomp.dev reported ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 moved from 0.00% to 100.00% (+432 bytes), ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 from 98.62% to 100.00% (+5 bytes), ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240 from 98.75% to 100.00% (+4 bytes), plus .sdata2 from 57.14% to 84.21% (+11 bytes).
smart_moves: Accepted a less artificial goto-less version after review while still preserving the match; the final form uses a compound condition with fp2 = GET_FIGHTER(gobj) rather than a visible goto path.
Kept the public/named ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot wrapper while moving the actual matched body into ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30, minimizing call-site churn.
Centralized address-named ftKb_Init globals in ftKb_Init.h instead of leaving ad hoc local externs in ftkirbyspecialfox.c.
Used explicit Fox/Falco hat-kind switches in the functions where codegen needed them, even though that repeats a small blaster_id selection block.
Responded to maintainer feedback by not leaving the intermediate raw __assert/string-extern form for the blaster creation failure path; project-preferred OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage was preserved.
decomp_lessons: A match can still be reviewed as too 'fake' if the structure is visibly unlikely original source, especially a goto used only to force codegen. If an equivalent structured form matches, prefer that over adding a fake-match comment.
PAD_STACK was discussed as technically artificial but widely present and expected to be cleaned up near the end of decomp; do not treat every PAD_STACK as requiring a fake-match warning by itself.

Preserve project-level readability wrappers like OSReport and HSD_ASSERT when reviewers identify them as the proper source-level form; do not lower them to __assert or extern string symbols just for convenience unless unavoidable.

The author noted that the temporary fp2 variable suggested an inlined helper, but factoring it out did not restore the match. Treat such inlining hypotheses as tentative unless the diff/review proves the original source shape.
For shared address-named data, prefer header declarations with address comments over repeated local externs; this PR added ftKb_Init_804D3DB8/804D3DC0/804D9478/804D9480 to ftKb_Init.h.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-Fox special symbols use the ftKb_SpecialNFx_* prefix, with ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot as the descriptive wrapper and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 as the address-style matched function.

ftKb_DatAttrs fields distinguish Fox and Falco data with specialn_fx_* versus specialn_fc_* names, e.g. specialn_fx_launch_angle, specialn_fc_launch_angle, specialn_fx_blaster_projectile_id, and specialn_fc_blaster_projectile_id.
Unknown ftKb_Init data keeps address-derived names such as ftKb_Init_804D3DB8, ftKb_Init_804D3DC0, ftKb_Init_804D9478, and ftKb_Init_804D9480, declared in the init header with address comments.
Hat-kind dispatch uses FTKIND_FOX and FTKIND_FALCO, while blaster attachment uses the existing part enum FtPart_R3rdNa.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK(4) was needed in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30's local layout.
The final goto-less match uses assignment in a condition: (gobj != NULL) && (fp2 = GET_FIGHTER(gobj)) != NULL.
A local f64 launchAngle and explicit M_PI - attribute calculation are used for the facing-direction-dependent projectile angle.
The SFX array index uses the boolean expression [-1 == fp->facing_dir] against two-entry u32 arrays, matching a compact compare-to-index idiom.

The small blaster_id switch was duplicated in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240 instead of using the previous inline ftKbGetBlasterId helper, apparently to satisfy exact codegen for those near-matching functions.
The projectile spawn position is produced through lb_8000B1CC on fp2->parts[FtPart_R3rdNa].joint with a fixed Vec3 local, then sp2C.z is forced to zero before spawning the laser.
review_feedback: ThePlayerRolo initially called the goto-based implementation a fake match and suggested adding a decomp.me scratch/comment if it could not be solved.

lukechampine questioned what counts as fake, compared the issue to M2C_FIELD, comma assignments, and PAD_STACK, and proposed a cleaner goto-less alternative while noting the result still might not be original source.
ThePlayerRolo clarified that the goto was the concern because the original likely did not use one; they also said PAD_STACK is so widespread that it is likely an end-of-decomp cleanup concern.
jurrejelle argued that a fake-match comment would not add much here, because the suspicious goto would be obvious, and said the cleaner 'less fake' option was good enough for now.
lukechampine then pushed the goto-less version.
PsiLupan requested not undoing proper OSReport/HSD_ASSERT usage in both ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240; the final merged code preserved the macro-style failure paths.
follow_up_queries: Find other Kirby copied-Fox/Falco SpecialN functions still using /// # placeholders or wrapper-only stubs.
Search for remaining local extern declarations of ftKb_Init_804D* data that should be promoted to ftKb_Init.h.
Look for other fake-match review discussions involving goto, M2C_FIELD, comma assignments, or PAD_STACK to derive project style rules.
Audit other places where OSReport/HSD_ASSERT were lowered to raw __assert or extern strings and consider restoring the preferred macros.
After matching cleanup is allowed, revisit duplicated Fox/Falco blaster_id switches and the fp2/GET_FIGHTER pattern to see whether a real helper can be recovered without breaking matches.
